name: Reserve Bank of India (RBI)
description: India's central bank responsible for monetary policy, inflation targeting (4% CPI ± 2%), financial stability, and forex reserve management. In an oil shock scenario, the RBI faces the classic impossible trinity — managing inflation, supporting growth, and defending the currency simultaneously.
objectives: Contain inflation within the 2-6% target band despite supply-side oil shock
Maintain orderly forex markets and prevent disruptive rupee depreciation
Preserve financial system stability — banks, NBFCs, and capital markets
Support economic growth without fuelling inflationary expectations
constraints: Inflation targeting mandate — CPI already at 5.5%, oil shock could push to 8-10%
Rate hikes to fight inflation would slow GDP growth (currently ~6.5%)
Forex reserves ($620B) are large but defending rupee burns reserves quickly
Capital outflows from FPIs during geopolitical risk reduce market liquidity
Transmission lag — rate changes take 6-9 months to affect real economy
Cannot control supply-side inflation with demand-side monetary tools

financial_stability.oil_company_borrowings_risk: Higher working capital needs from OMCs could stress banking system

policy_tools_available: Adjust repo rate (hike to fight inflation, cut to support growth)
Open market operations (OMO) — buy/sell government securities
Forex market intervention — sell USD from reserves to defend rupee
Special dollar swap windows for oil marketing companies
Forward guidance and communication to anchor expectations
Regulatory forbearance for stressed sectors (aviation, transport)

historical_precedent.2022_ukraine_shock: RBI hiked repo 250bps, spent $70B defending rupee, inflation peaked at 7.8%
historical_precedent.2008_oil_shock: Crude at $147 — RBI raised rates, rupee fell 25%, inflation hit 12%
historical_precedent.2014_oil_crash: Crude fell to $45 — RBI cut rates 150bps, rupee stabilised, growth recovered
